text:
Thank you. Yeah, I think it's all right. Thank you, Brother Baxter. Good evening, friends. Very happy to be here again this evening to give our expressions of love and our adoration of our heart to the worshippers of Jesus Christ. And I trust that this will be a great evening. If it was any more than last evening, I don't know whether we can stand it or not.
Of all my ministerial life, I never seen any more of a quick falling of the Spirit of God than I did last evening. I went completely numb, just went out. I looked down, and coming over the building, and that Light just spread the whole entire building. And I tried to say something; I just couldn't say no more. And the next I knowed, there was some ushers talking to me somewhere, ever which way the parking grounds are.
text_no:
Takk, Bror Baxter. God kveld, venner. Det er en glede å være her igjen i kveld for å uttrykke vår kjærlighet og vår hjertefulle tilbedelse til Jesus Kristus. Jeg håper at dette blir en storartet kveld. Hvis det blir noe mer enn i går kveld, vet jeg ikke om vi kan tåle det.
I løpet av min tjeneste har jeg aldri opplevd en raskere nærværelse av Guds Ånd enn i går kveld. Jeg ble helt nummen, gikk helt ut. Jeg så ned og så at Lyset fylte hele bygningen. Jeg forsøkte å si noe, men ordene kom ikke. Det neste jeg visste, var at noen hjelpere snakket til meg, og jeg var på parkeringsplassen.

text:
Mr. Baxter, after he come in, I begin to get to myself a little. And he said, "Brother Branham, this is the first time in my life that I ever--was ever floored down like that." And I… Just how wonderful that God was dealing with us. I've never been able…
Two, three nights, I've been trying to do this, and I packed it in my pocket. I want to express my thanks for the kindness, some presents some people sent me. And I appreciate anything that anyone does.
When I first came in, there was a bunch--a bouquet of flowers, two of them sitting on the table, sent from the D-e-n-n-e-r-s, Denners, I suppose of the city. They're here tonight, they may not be. If they are, I want to express my thanks to you for those beautiful flowers: the Denners.
text_no:
Mr. Baxter, etter at han kom inn, begynte jeg å komme til meg selv litt. Han sa: "Bror Branham, dette er første gang i mitt liv at jeg noen gang har blitt lammet slik." Og jeg... hvor vidunderlig det er at Gud handler med oss. Jeg har aldri vært i stand til...
I to-tre netter har jeg prøvd å gjøre dette, og jeg har hatt det i lommen min. Jeg vil uttrykke min takknemlighet for gavene enkelte har sendt meg. Jeg setter pris på alt hva noen gjør.
Da jeg først kom inn, var det en bukett med blomster, to faktisk, som sto på bordet. De ble sendt fra D-e-n-n-e-r-s, antakelig Denners fra byen. Om de er her i kveld, eller ikke, vil jeg uttrykke min takknemlighet for disse vakre blomstene: fra Denners.

text:
And then the next was, my boy gave me a letter which had a love offering in it of five dollars, that come from Brother S-a-n-d-r-e-s-k-y. He said, "I'd love to get my arms around you and hug you." Well, brother, that's a mutual feeling. I would sure like the same thing.
And today, when my little boy--boy came in, he had two big jars of honey, and two boxes of these red raspberries, I suppose they are. Well, I guess the… You don't know how I appreciate it. I sure love honey. I love raspberries too. Mix them together and I really got them then, haven't I.
text_no:
Min sønn ga meg en gave på fem dollar fra Bror Sandresky. Han skrev i brevet: "Jeg skulle ønske jeg kunne gi deg en klem." Det er en følelse jeg deler. Jeg ville også gjerne klemmet ham.
I dag kom min sønn inn med to store glass honning og to esker med røde bringebær. Jeg setter stor pris på det. Jeg elsker både honning og bringebær. Kombinert blir det virkelig en herlig blanding.

text:
One of the cutest little things that I ever seen was one day on a fishing trip in the mountains. I just love to fish and hunt. And I was up in the mountains fishing; I'd had my tent. And it was early spring, trout fishing, just trying to get away from the crowds for a little while. And I love pancakes. And I guess I got a lot of fellow citizens on that.
And so, you know, I was… I like to put honey on them. And I have one theory I still stay with; I'm a Baptist. I don't sprinkle them; I really pour it on them. So I have it covered all over, baptizing them. And always take a large bucket of honey, you know.
And one day coming around, I heard a noise. And there'd been an old mother bear and her two cubs had got in my tent. They just tore it all to pieces. And I didn't have my camera. And the cutest little act… There's one of the little fellows run away with the mother. But the other little fellow sat there. He'd got the lid off that bucket of honey. And he taken his paw and push down it like that, and then come up and just lick his hands. And he was mo… He was honey from his feet to his head. Just so… He looked around, it was all in his eyes, you know. And he looked at me like that, you know. No condemnation to him. He just stuck his hand back down in the bucket and got some more.
text_no:
En av de mest sjarmerende tingene jeg noen gang har sett, skjedde en dag på en fisketur i fjellene. Jeg elsker å fiske og jakte. Jeg var oppe i fjellene for å fiske, med teltet mitt. Det var tidlig vår, ørretfiske, og jeg ønsket å komme meg bort fra folkemengdene en stund. Jeg elsker pannekaker, og jeg vet at mange av mine medborgere også gjør det.
Jeg liker å ha honning på dem, og jeg holder meg fortsatt til én teori; jeg er en baptist. Jeg drysser ikke honningen, jeg heller ordentlig på. Jeg dekker dem helt til, som en dåp. Jeg tar alltid med en stor bøtte honning, vet du.
En dag, mens jeg var ute, hørte jeg en lyd. En gammel binne og hennes to bjørneunger hadde kommet seg inn i teltet mitt og revet det i stykker. Jeg hadde ikke med meg kameraet mitt. En av de søteste tingene jeg så den dagen, var en av de små bjørnungene som løp etter moren sin. Men den andre ungen satt der. Han hadde fått lokket av bøtten med honning. Han dyppet labben sin nedi, trakk opp og slikket hendene sine. Han var dekket av honning fra føttene til hodet. Han så rundt seg, det var honning til og med i øynene hans. Han så på meg uten noen form for skyldfølelse. Han bare stakk hånden tilbake i bøtten og tok litt mer.

text:
And I thought that was just like an old fashion Holy Ghost meeting. You don't care who's around as long as you got your hand in the honey. Is that right? Yes, sir. Licking it around, so no condemnation to them that's licking, you know. It's all right.
But they was sure having a grand time. Well, I looked at the little fellow. I laughed till my sides hurt, and I just walked away. And I still was out the bucket of honey, letting him lick.
So I appreciate that very much. And we'll do justice to that honey in the morning. So thank you very much.
text_no:
Og jeg tenkte at dette var som et gammeldags møte med Den Hellige Ånd. Du bryr Deg ikke om hvem som er rundt Deg, så lenge Du har hånden Din i honningen. Er det ikke riktig? Ja, det stemmer. Du slikker det i Deg uten fordømmelse, vet Du. Det er helt greit.
Men de hadde det virkelig fantastisk. Jeg så på den lille fyren og lo så hardt at sidene mine gjorde vondt, og så gikk jeg bare videre. Og han var fortsatt ved honningbøtta, og slikket.
Så jeg setter stor pris på det. Vi skal nyte den honningen i morgen tidlig. Tusen takk.

text:
And now, the services… It's awfully warm in here. And I just got to hear just a portion of your broadcast. There's… They come and got me. And it sure sound lovely, the people playing and singing, and those people on that--this thing here, you know. I'm not very much a musician, but… And on the organ and that trumpet or ever what it was a playing; it was really pretty, "Onward Christian Soldiers," and all your voices blending in.
I had to shed a little tear too, because it just made my heart thrill to think that someday, the saints of God will go marching up to glory. And to see that great time…
text_no:
Nå til møtene... Det er fryktelig varmt her inne. Jeg hørte bare en liten del av sendingen deres. De kom og hentet meg, men det hørtes virkelig nydelig ut med folk som spilte og sang, og de på dette instrumentet her, vet du. Jeg er ikke mye til musiker, men... På orgelet og den trompeten eller hva det nå var som spilte; det var virkelig vakkert, "Fram Kristi Stridsmenn," og alle stemmene deres som smeltet sammen.
Jeg måtte felle en liten tåre også, fordi det virkelig gledet hjertet mitt å tenke på at en dag vil Guds hellige marsjere opp til herligheten. Å se for seg den store tiden...

text:
Brother Baxter met me this afternoon, said, "Brother Branham, you're to preach for tomorrow."
Oh, my. I said, "Brother Baxter, I can't preach." And he said… after a man like that being preaching.
And he said… Then he met me just a few minutes ago, and said, "The people like for you to tell your life story tomorrow." So… Oh, I can do that, by grace of God.
And I want to be here tomorrow afternoon around two-thirty, I suppose. And I expect to see you all. Be just as quickly as I can. Bring the sinner and the unrepented out tomorrow, so that I--we can make an altar call, and maybe God will give us many souls for His glory.
text_no:
Bror Baxter møtte meg i ettermiddag og sa: "Bror Branham, du skal forkynne i morgen."
Åh, tenkte jeg. Jeg sa: "Bror Baxter, jeg kan ikke forkynne." Etter at en mann som ham hadde forkynt, sa han...
Så møtte han meg igjen for bare få minutter siden og sa: "Folk ønsker at du forteller din livshistorie i morgen." Det kan jeg gjøre, ved Guds nåde.
Jeg vil være her i morgen ettermiddag rundt halv tre, antar jeg. Jeg forventer å se dere alle. Kom så tidlig som mulig. Ta med syndere og de uomvendte i morgen, så vi kan ha en alterkall, og kanskje vil Gud gi oss mange sjeler til Hans ære.

text:
Now, for just a portion of time, I want to read some of the Word, and then just pass a few comments, and maybe give a testimony, and try to get into the prayer line as quick as possible. And… Because they told me, that coming down tonight (they come and got me), that the auditorium had been packed out since about five o'clock. And someone said they was going to be here at six in the morning to see if they could get a seat up front.
So, we're sorry we haven't any way to take care of any more. The fire laws just allows to have so many in here. But while we're here together, I know you've had a glorious time. If we should say, "Amen," and go home, it would be wonderful.
But now, let's just, you and I, ask God, and believe that God will answer our prayer. And let's ask God to heal this rows of people through here tonight, every one.
text_no:
For en liten stund ønsker jeg å lese noe fra Ordet, gi noen kommentarer, kanskje komme med et vitnesbyrd, og forsøke å komme inn i bønnekøen så raskt som mulig. De fortalte meg at auditoriet var fullt allerede fra klokken fem i ettermiddag. Noen sa at de ville komme hit klokken seks i morgentid for å sikre seg en plass foran.
Vi beklager at vi ikke kan ta imot flere. Brannforskriftene tillater kun et visst antall personer her inne. Mens vi er samlet, vet jeg at dere har hatt en herlig tid. Skulle vi si "Amen" og gå hjem nå, ville det vært fantastisk.
La oss nå be Gud, og tro at Han vil svare på våre bønner. La oss be om at Gud helbreder alle som er her i kveld.

text:
I… When Brother Baxter came in, and my boy was kind of talking to me. I could hear him in the distance last night. He kept getting closer. I knew I was coming to myself. And he said, "Daddy, are you all right?"
I said, "Yes."
I never will forget the first time the boy ever seen the Angel of the Lord at Vandalia, Illinois. And I'd had a meeting, and I made a challenge to the people, "Just bring me anybody you want to, and let me have enough time with them. They'll be healed." And they brought me a blind boy, was born blind. And I stayed with that boy about an hour and forty minutes. And he walked off the platform with his sight.
And he came back, and he picked up my tie so speak… He said, "Mother, is that what you… What color you call that?" And the boy was born blind. And he was just so enthused, and the people screaming and going on.
text_no:
Da Bror Baxter kom inn og min gutt snakket litt til meg, kunne jeg høre ham på avstand i går kveld. Han kom stadig nærmere, og jeg visste at jeg begynte å komme til meg selv. Han spurte: "Pappa, går det bra?"
Jeg svarte: "Ja."
Jeg vil aldri glemme første gang gutten så Herrens Engel i Vandalia, Illinois. Jeg hadde hatt et møte og utfordret folket: "Bring hvem som helst til meg, og gi meg nok tid med dem, så vil de bli helbredet." De brakte meg en blind gutt som var født blind. Jeg brukte omtrent en time og førti minutter med ham. Han gikk av plattformen med synet sitt.
Gutten kom tilbake og løftet opp slipset mitt. Han spurte: "Mor, hva kaller du den fargen?" Han var født blind, og var så entusiastisk. Folk skrek og jublet.

text:
And when I went home that night, we was in the hotel. And I was been praying, and I was almost asleep. And I woke up, and I knew He was in the room. I couldn't tell from where He was coming. But I waited a few minutes, and I knew It was coming over this a way, coming across towards the bed.
I got out of the bed. My brother and my little boy was next to me in another bed. And I felt It coming, and I got out, and I knelt down in the floor, and started praying. He got real close. I never heard no voice nor nothing. I waited a little bit. It got closer. And I kept speaking, "What would the Lord have me to know?"
And a voice begin to speak in the room and told me not to do that, not to do that. And just… My commission was to pray for the sick, and not to do that. And then when I opened my eyes, there He was, just above the bed whirling around and around.
And I said, "Do You mind if my brother and little son could see You?"
And He never made any answer back. It was done leaving me, the anointing. I seen It right off from me. And I kept my eye on Him, and He never answered me, so I thought maybe He didn't care. I picked up a pillow, and threw it over on the bed, and it woke up my brother. He said, "What do you want?"
I said, "Wake up Billy."
And he said, "Billy, your dad wants you."
And as soon as he looked back, he let out a scream. He saw It. He begin screaming. And my little boy jumped over in the bed with me, and begin hugging me around the neck, saying, "Daddy, daddy, don't let that get me. Don't let that get me."
I said, "Why, honey, that ain't what… That won't hurt you." I said, "That's the Angel of the Lord that leads daddy."
text_no:
Da jeg kom hjem den kvelden, var vi på hotellet. Jeg hadde bedt og var nesten i ferd med å sovne. Jeg våknet og følte at Han var i rommet. Jeg kunne ikke si hvor Han kom fra, men etter noen minutter skjønte jeg at Han nærmet seg sengen.
Jeg stod opp av sengen. Min bror og min lille gutt lå i en annen seng ved siden av meg. Da jeg følte Hans nærvær sterkere, gikk jeg ut av sengen, knelte ned på gulvet og begynte å be. Han kom veldig nær. Jeg hørte ingen stemme eller noe, men ventet litt til, og Han kom enda nærmere. Jeg fortsatte å spørre: "Hva vil Herren at jeg skal vite?"
En stemme begynte å tale i rommet og ba meg om å ikke gjøre det og ikke gjøre det. Min oppgave var å be for de syke, ikke gjøre det. Da jeg åpnet øynene, så jeg Ham, rett over sengen, svevende rundt og rundt.
Jeg spurte: "Gjør det noe om min bror og lille sønn kan se Deg?"
Han svarte ikke. Salvelsen begynte å forlate meg, jeg kunne kjenne det. Jeg fulgte Ham med blikket, men Han ga ingen svar, så jeg trodde kanskje det var greit. Jeg tok opp en pute og kastet den over på sengen, noe som vekket min bror. Han spurte: "Hva vil du?"
Jeg sa: "Vekke Billy."
Han sa: "Billy, faren din vil snakke med deg."
Så snart han snudde seg, skrek han. Han så Ham. Han begynte å skrike. Min lille gutt hoppet over i sengen min og begynte å klemme meg rundt halsen, og sa: "Pappa, pappa, ikke la den ta meg. Ikke la den ta meg."
Jeg sa: "Nei, kjære, det der vil ikke... Det vil ikke skade deg." Jeg sa: "Det er Herrens engel som leder pappa."

text:
Billy's an orphan. His mother died when he was just about eighteen months old. And he's a little orphan child. And I been daddy and mother both to him. And sometimes in going away, the little fellow used to stand at the airport and cry and go on. He said, "Daddy, don't leave me." Said, "What have I got on earth but you?" Said, "Mother's gone and everything." And said, "If something happens to you, what will happen to me?"
So that's not easy to leave your children like that. But Jesus said, "Whosoever will not forsake his… everything and follow Me, not worthy to be called My disciple." No matter what we do, we'll never be worthy to be that. But we like to do our part.
So after that night, the little boy was consoled then when he got to see the Angel of the Lord for the first time. He never worried no more when I left.
text_no:
Billy er foreldreløs. Moren hans døde da han var rundt atten måneder gammel. Og han er et lite foreldreløst barn. Jeg har vært både far og mor for ham. Når jeg måtte reise bort, pleide den lille gutten å stå ved flyplassen og gråte. Han sa: "Pappa, ikke forlat meg. Hva har jeg på jorden utenom deg?" Han fortsatte: "Mor er borte, og alt annet også. Hvis noe skjer med deg, hva skal jeg gjøre da?"
Det er ikke lett å forlate barna sine slik. Men Jesus sa: "Den som ikke forlater alt han har, og følger Meg, er ikke verdig til å være Min disippel." Uansett hva vi gjør, vil vi aldri være verdige til det, men vi prøver å gjøre vår del.
Etter den natten ble den lille gutten trøstet da han for første gang fikk se Herrens Engel. Han bekymret seg aldri mer når jeg dro.

text:
Now, the reading of the Scripture for the text tonight is found in the 2nd chapter of Saint Luke, you who would like to refer to it and would read with me. I…
You know, God's Word can't fail, can it. And now, I tell you, dear Christian friends, the anointing of the Angel of God is so strong, I can hardly look out over this audience. Now, that's right. And I don't like for it to be like that right to the beginning. And I want to talk just a little while on the Scripture. Wasn't aiming to do it, just give a testimony. But I'll try to talk just a moment.
Listen to the reading, beginning with the 25th verse:
… behold, there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon; … the same man was just … devout, waiting for the consolation of Israel: and the Holy Ghost was upon him.
And it was revealed unto him by the Holy Ghost, that he should not see death, before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
And he came by the Spirit into the temple: and when the parents brought in the child Jesus, to do for him after the custom of the law,
Then took he him up in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
Lord, now let thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word:
For mine eyes have seen thy salvation.
text_no:
Lesningen av Skriften for kveldens tekst er hentet fra det andre kapittelet av Evangeliet etter Lukas. Dere som ønsker å slå opp og lese med meg, kan gjøre det nå.
Guds Ord kan ikke feile, kan det vel? Kjære kristne venner, salvelsen fra Guds engel er så sterk at jeg knapt kan se ut over forsamlingen. Selvsagt, og jeg liker ikke at det er slik helt fra begynnelsen. Jeg vil gjerne snakke litt om Skriften. Jeg hadde egentlig ikke tenkt å gjøre det, bare avlegge et vitnesbyrd. Men jeg skal prøve å snakke et øyeblikk.
Lytt til lesningen, begynner med det 25. verset:
"Og se, det var en mann i Jerusalem, som het Simeon, og denne mannen var rettferdig og gudfryktig og ventet på Israels trøst, og Den Hellige Ånd var over ham.  
Og det var blitt åpenbart for ham av Den Hellige Ånd, at han ikke skulle se døden før han hadde sett Herrens Kristus.  
Han kom, drevet av Ånden, inn i templet. Da foreldrene førte barnet Jesus inn for å gjøre med Ham som skikken var etter loven,  
tok også han Ham opp i sine armer, lovpriste Gud og sa:  
Herre, nå lar du Din tjener fare herfra i fred, etter Ditt Ord,  
for mine øyne har sett Din frelse."

text:
Let's bow our heads. Father, the hours are moving on. Help us, Almighty God, to know Your will. There are many gathered tonight. The building is hot. Oh, we believe the harvest is ripe; the fields are ready. Thrust in Thy sickle tonight, Lord; reap. May the wine be brought from the grapes tonight, till each believer will become, as it were, drunk upon the Spirit of God, knowing nothing but the presence of God, and His power, His redeeming blessings upon us to redeem us from our sickness, from our sins. Grant these blessings, Lord.
And as Thy servant has determined by Thy grace, if possible, to speak a few words, help me, dear God. And may the Holy Spirit take the Word of God and plant it right into the heart where it belongs. And may, before this service is ended, may there be people all over the floor everywhere. May the people be up off of these cots and stretchers. May the blind be laying their canes back. May the crippled lay down their canes and crutches, and walk out. May those with heart trouble have it no more, with the cancers…
Lord, we thank Thee for Your visit last night. And now, Lord, maybe a new fresh group is in tonight. And I pray that You'll heal them, every one, and make it manifested to them by the power of the resurrected Christ, for we ask it in the name of Jesus Christ, the beloved Son of God. Amen.
text_no:
La oss bøye hodene. Far, timene går. Hjelp oss, Allmektige Gud, å kjenne Din vilje. Mange har samlet seg i kveld, og bygningen er varm. Åh, vi tror høsten er moden; markene er klare. Sett Din sigd i arbeidet i kveld, Herre; høst. Måtte vinen bringes fra druene i kveld, så hver troende blir, som det var, beruset av Guds Ånd, uten å kjenne annet enn Guds nærvær, Hans kraft og Hans forløsende velsignelser over oss for å befri oss fra vår sykdom, fra våre synder. Gi oss disse velsignelsene, Herre.
Og som Din tjener har bestemt ved Din nåde, om mulig, å tale noen ord, hjelp meg, kjære Gud. Måtte Den Hellige Ånd ta Guds Ord og plante det direkte i hjertet hvor det hører hjemme. La det, før dette møtet er avsluttet, være mennesker over hele gulvet. Måtte folk reise seg fra disse bårene og bårdene. Måtte de blinde legge fra seg stokkene. Måtte de lamme legge bort krykker og gå ut. Måtte de med hjertetrøbbel bli helbredet, og de med kreft...
Herre, vi takker Deg for Ditt besøk i går kveld. Og nå, Herre, kanskje en ny, frisk gruppe er her i kveld. Jeg ber om at Du helbreder dem, hver og en, og gjør det klart for dem gjennom kraften til den oppstandne Kristus, for vi ber om dette i Jesu Kristi navn, Guds elskede Sønn. Amen.

text:
Now quickly, your undivided attention. Faith cometh by hearing, and hearing of the Word. If we'd say for a text, which it will not be, that I would say tonight, "Expectations."
You know, usually you get what you expect, don't you? What you think you're going… If you come to the meeting, just say, "I believe I'll go down. There's something wrong with that kind of a meeting. I'm going down. I'll see what's wrong with it." You will. The devil will be sure that you see that.
But if you come down to see what's good about it, God will show you what's good about it. See? If you come down, saying, "Well, if I… I don't believe I'll get healed. I'll go down maybe see if I can get a prayer card. But if I don't, well, they won't…" No, that's just what you'll get, just see… And just whatever you expect. Don't you believe that?
When you come, expect this: let's expect this tonight. Let's expect a great outpouring again. I believe if God would repeat that again tonight… I don't know that He will. Twice I've asked Him. I guess Brother Baxter told you the phenomenal of last night, of how that I never knew.
text_no:
Vær oppmerksom nå. Troen kommer av å høre, og høre av Ordet. La oss anta at kveldens tema er "Forventninger."
Som regel får man det man forventer, ikke sant? Hvis du kommer til møtet og tenker: "Jeg tror jeg skal dra ned. Det er noe galt med dette møtet. Jeg går ned for å finne ut hva som er feil." Da vil du finne noe galt. Djevelen vil sørge for at du ser det.
Men hvis du kommer for å finne det gode ved møtet, vil Gud vise deg det. Hvis du tenker: "Jeg tror ikke jeg blir helbredet. Jeg går kanskje ned for å få et bønnekort. Men hvis ikke, så…" Ja, da er det akkurat det du vil få. Du får det du forventer. Ikke sant?
La oss forvente dette i kveld: La oss forvente en stor utgytelse igjen. Jeg tror Gud kan gjenta det i kveld. Jeg vet ikke om Han vil. Jeg har spurt Ham to ganger. Jeg antar at Bror Baxter fortalte dere om det fenomenet som skjedde i går kveld, om hvordan jeg ikke visste det.

text:
Then the Angel of the Lord met me in the room, and I asked Him to do something outstanding. And a few nights ago, I asked Him if He would do something outstanding for us that night, and He did. He healed a woman that was deaf for years, and taken a woman out of a wheelchair. And I forget what all He done. And now, I'm asking again tonight to do something special for us. And last night I asked Him.
I trust that my heavenly Father won't be angry with me for asking these special blessings. For it's written in the Word, "Except you see signs or miracles, you won't believe." And we don't want to be classed amongst such people as that. Why, we're people who believe anyhow. See, we believe anyhow, and we want to accept it.
text_no:
Da møtte Herrens Engel meg i rommet, og jeg ba Ham om å gjøre noe spesielt. For noen netter siden ba jeg Ham om å gjøre noe bemerkelsesverdig for oss den kvelden, og det gjorde Han. Han helbredet en kvinne som hadde vært døv i årevis og hjalp en annen kvinne ut av en rullestol. Jeg husker ikke alt Han gjorde. Nå ber jeg igjen i kveld om at Han skal gjøre noe spesielt for oss. I går kveld ba jeg Ham om det samme.
Jeg stoler på at min himmelske Far ikke blir sint på meg for å be om disse spesielle velsignelsene. For det står skrevet i Ordet: "Uten tegn eller mirakler, vil dere ikke tro." Vi ønsker ikke å bli klassifisert blant slike mennesker. Vi er folk som tror uansett. Vi tror uansett og ønsker å ta imot det.

text:
Last night I was wondering how there could be anyone left in the building that wasn't healed. Now, it may not be visible. Angels of God and visions appear before people, and sometime it's not for everybody. Is that right?
Star. It passed by every observatory, passed by all the stargazers and everything, passed right on by the spiritual people, passed right on over the temple; and no one saw it but the Magis. It was for them to see it. Do you believe they saw it? I do, for the Bible said they did. See? And if it be possible that the Angel of God could be visible to some and not visible to the others.
text_no:
I går kveld undret jeg meg over hvordan det kunne være noen igjen i bygningen som ikke var helbredet. Det kan være at det ikke er synlig for alle. Guds engler og visjoner kan vise seg for enkelte mennesker, men det er ikke alltid for alle. Er det ikke riktig?
Stjernen. Den passerte forbi alle observatorier og stjernetydere, forbi de åndelige menneskene og rett over tempelet; og ingen så den bortsett fra vismennene. Det var bestemt at de skulle se den. Tror dere de så den? Det gjør jeg, for Bibelen sier de gjorde det. Ser dere? Det er mulig at Guds engel kan være synlig for noen og usynlig for andre.

text:
For instance one day when I was baptizing. There was around ten thousand people, I guess, at the bank. And there was at least seven thousand of them that seen the Angel of the Lord, and say the other three thousand never saw It. They said, "Well, I didn't see it. I heard you hollering, and seen you raise your hand, and everybody screaming, but I didn't see nothing." You see?
And that's the way it was when the picture was taken. Hundreds and hundreds saw It, and others didn't. Some of them in the Bible times, it was the same way. It's today.
Magis were expecting to see the Star of Jacob rise. They were from the East, and they'd listened to Balaam's prophecy, which said that there would be a Star of Jacob rise. And they were looking for it. They was expecting it. And they were the ones who seen it.
text_no:
For eksempel, en dag da jeg døpte. Det var rundt ti tusen mennesker ved elvebredden, tror jeg. Minst syv tusen av dem så Herrens Engel, men de andre tre tusen så den ikke. De sa: "Vel, jeg så det ikke. Jeg hørte deg rope, så deg løfte hånden og alle skrike, men jeg så ingenting." 
Slik var det da bildet ble tatt. Hundrevis så Det, mens andre ikke gjorde det. Det var det samme i Bibelens tid. Og det er slik i dag.
De vise ventet på å se Jakobs Stjerne stige. De kom fra Østen og hadde hørt på Bileams profeti, som sa at Jakobs Stjerne skulle stige. De lette etter den. De forventet den. Og det var de som så den.

text:
And how many in here tonight is expecting to be healed tonight, let's see your hands? Well, you're not going to be disappointed then. If you… You just can't be disappointed if you're expecting it. If you're not expecting it, you say, "Well, I'll wait maybe tomorrow afternoon; I'll get it tomorrow afternoon." Well, that's when you'll get it.
You say, "Well, I don't know. If He don't come to me personally, I don't believe I'll get it." Well, that's…
Jesus said, "According to your faith, be it unto you." Is that right?
A lady said not long ago… I went into a place to pray for a boy that had tubercular meningitis. He was dying. And the person said, "Oh, Brother Branham," said, "my," said, "you'll get meningitis." She said, "I run my children out. I give them vaccinations."
I said, "Such faith as that would give them meningitis." That's right.
You must believe. All things are possible to him that believes. Be expecting God to deliver you, for He promised it. And as soon as your faith can catch it, it's gone away. And that's right. Now, I was… Whatever you expect, that's what God will do.
text_no:
Hvor mange her i kveld forventer å bli helbredet? La meg se hendene deres. Vel, dere kommer ikke til å bli skuffet. Hvis dere forventer det, kan dere ikke bli skuffet. Hvis dere ikke forventer det og sier, "Vel, kanskje i morgen ettermiddag; jeg får det i morgen ettermiddag," så er det da dere vil få det. 
Hvis dere sier, "Vel, jeg vet ikke. Hvis Han ikke kommer til meg personlig, tror jeg ikke jeg vil få det," så er det…
Jesus sa, "Etter din tro, la det skje deg." Er det riktig?
En dame sa ikke for lenge siden… Jeg gikk inn et sted for å be for en gutt som hadde tuberkuløs meningitt. Han var døende. Og personen sa, "Å, Bror Branham," sa, "du vil få meningitt." Hun sa, "Jeg jager barna mine ut. Jeg gir dem vaksiner."
Jeg sa, "Med slik tro som det, vil du gi dem meningitt." Det er riktig.
Du må tro. Alle ting er mulig for den som tror. Forvent at Gud vil frelse deg, for Han har lovet det. Og så snart din tro kan gripe det, er det borte. Det er riktig. Uansett hva du forventer, det er hva Gud vil gjøre.

text:
I was thinking of Simeon. In his day he was an old man. We'd call him maybe in them days an old sage. He's just… Try to picture him with long white beard. And he was a wonderful old saint of God. Now, the Bible said the Holy Ghost was upon him. Do you believe that? Yes, he… The Holy Ghost was on him. The…
Now, always God has had someone who believed His Word. Somebody's going to expect God to fulfill His Word, while others pass it by. Now, as it was in that day, so is it now. The church has gotten formal and got away from God. And they thought Jesus, their Christ, never would come; perhaps, maybe being many ages away. But just when they was expecting it to be way away, He was right at hand.
text_no:
Jeg tenkte på Simeon. På hans tid var han en gammel mann. Vi ville kanskje kalt ham en gammel vismann i de dager. Prøv å se for deg ham med langt, hvitt skjegg. Han var en fantastisk gammel hellig mann av Gud. Bibelen sier at Den Hellige Ånd var over ham. Tror du det? Ja, Den Hellige Ånd var over ham.
Gud har alltid hatt noen som trodde på Hans Ord. Noen forventer at Gud skal oppfylle Sitt Ord, mens andre overser det. Som det var den gang, er det også nå. Menigheten har blitt formell og fjernet seg fra Gud. De trodde kanskje at Jesus, deres Kristus, aldri ville komme, eller at det ville ta mange tidsaldre. Men akkurat når de forventet at det skulle være langt frem i tid, var Han rett ved hånden.

text:
Now, let's take this case now of Simeon. Now, he was a righteous old man. He believed that God was going to let him see the Christ before he saw death. Now, let's say he's eighty something years old. And then He had another witness there. There was Anna the prophetess at the temple, John the Baptist, and Joseph and Mary, and many of them: a remnant.
God has always had a remnant somewhere. And He's got a remnant tonight. Don't you believe that? Now, tonight, His remnant are composed of His body, His spiritual body here on earth, baptized people into the body making us the body of Christ.Now, that doesn't represent any certain church; it just represents all the baptized people of God into one Body. By not one church, but by one Spirit are we all baptized to one body and become members of the body of Christ.
text_no:
La oss ta eksempelet med Simeon. Han var en rettskaffen, gammel mann som trodde at Gud ville la ham se Kristus før han døde. Si at han var over åtti år gammel. Det fantes også et annet vitne: Anna profetinnen i tempelet, Johannes Døperen, samt Josef og Maria, og mange andre – en rest.
Gud har alltid hatt en rest et sted, og Han har en rest i kveld. Tror du ikke det? I kveld består Hans rest av Hans kropp, Hans åndelige kropp her på jorden, døpte mennesker innlemmet i kroppen som utgjør Kristi kropp. Dette representerer ikke en bestemt menighet, men alle døpte personer av Gud i én kropp. Ikke gjennom én menighet, men gjennom én Ånd er vi alle døpt til én kropp og blir medlemmer av Kristi kropp.

text:
Now, in them days I can see Simeon, the old man walking along down the street, and saying, "Well, I'm going to see the Christ before I die."
Could you imagine some of those scholars saying, "Don't pay no attention to the old fellow. I believe he's just… He's got old now, and his mind's kindly went away from him. And the poor old fellow, he's a good old man. But you know, an old man like that isn't going to… Why, we've looked for Him for age, and look at the condition of the world today. Why, we know He isn't coming now."
But Simeon had one reason that he believed that: the Holy Ghost revealed it to him. There's the thing. God had told Simeon that he wasn't going to see death until he had seen the Christ. And he believed what the Holy Ghost said was the truth.
Now, can you do that? Now, the same Holy Ghost that led Simeon, is the same Holy Ghost that's here tonight. There's not no two Holy Spirits, are they, just one Holy Spirit.
Now, look at him. Now, he had to believe something more than you believe, see. People are getting healed every night, every day, and all over the world, big healing campaigns everywhere. They're getting healed. But Jesus or Christ had never come to the earth. But it was prophesied that He would come, and Simeon had the promise of the Holy Spirit, that he was going to see Him.
text_no:
Den gamle Simeon vandret nedover gaten og sa: "Jeg skal se Kristus før jeg dør." Kan du forestille deg noen av de lærde som sa: "Ikke bry deg om den gamle karen. Jeg tror han har blitt gammel nå, og hodet hans har sviktet. Den stakkars gamle mannen, han er en god mann. Men du vet, en slik eldre mann kommer ikke til å... Vi har ventet på Ham i evigheter, og se på verdens tilstand i dag. Vi vet at Han ikke kommer nå."
Men Simeon hadde én grunn til å tro: Den Hellige Ånd hadde åpenbart det til ham. Gud hadde fortalt Simeon at han ikke skulle dø før han hadde sett Kristus, og han trodde at det Den Hellige Ånd sa, var sant.
Kan du gjøre det samme? Den samme Hellige Ånd som ledet Simeon, er den samme Hellige Ånd som er her i kveld. Det finnes ikke to Hellige Ånder, bare én.
Se på ham. Han måtte tro noe mer enn det du tror. Folk blir helbredet hver natt, hver dag, over hele verden, store helbredelseskampanjer overalt. De blir helbredet. Men Jesus, eller Kristus, hadde aldri kommet til jorden. Det var profetert at Han skulle komme, og Simeon hadde løftet fra Den Hellige Ånd om at han skulle se Ham.

text:
Now, the Bible said… I believe David said, "When the deep calleth to the deep…" Now, if there's a deep a calling, there's a deep to respond to it somewhere. Do you believe that? In other words like this, as I've often said, "Before there was a fin on a fish's back, there had to be a water first for him to use that fin, or he wouldn't have the fin." Is that right? If there's a tree to grow in the earth, there has to be an earth first for it to grow in, or there would be no tree.
And then… See, I love nature, to look at nature and see the sunsets and the mountains. There's something there that just calls out.
text_no:
Bibelen sier, og jeg tror det var David som sa, "Når dypet kaller på dypet..." Nå, hvis det er et dyp som kaller, finnes det et dyp et sted som svarer på det. Tror du det? Med andre ord, som jeg ofte har sagt, "Før det fantes en finne på en fisks rygg, måtte det først være vann for at fisken kunne bruke finnen, ellers ville den ikke hatt noen finne." Er det riktig? Hvis det er et tre som skal vokse på jorden, må det først være en jord for at det skal gro, ellers ville det ikke vært noe tre.
Og så, se, jeg elsker naturen – å se på naturen, solnedgangene og fjellene. Det er noe der som bare kaller på meg.

text:
Not long ago I was sitting on the porch. And wife and I, we were in a swing, swinging. The crowds had just got away. And some old sand cranes flew across, going down to the river. And they were calling. And the--a lady come up, give me a little card about like that, that had that sunset and evening star and one clear call for me.
And I looked at those old cranes, and I said, "Look, God has fed them all day long out in the swamps. They're going down to the falls now to get with their kindred and there be for the night."
Two little robins, my favorite birds, flew up, and went into their nest. They'd fed their young all day long. God had provided for them. And at nighttime, He had a way provided for them to roost in safety.
And I said, "Surely, someday when life is ended, and the sun of my life is setting, and your life, God has a way somewhere." There's something that tells us there's something out there: The deep calling to the deep.
text_no:
Nylig satt jeg på verandaen sammen med min kone. Vi satt på en huske og gynget. Mengden hadde nettopp forlatt oss. Noen gamle sandtraner fløy forbi, på vei mot elven, mens de ropte til hverandre. En dame kom bort og ga meg et lite kort med et bilde av en solnedgang, en kveldshimmel og en klar beskjed for meg.
Jeg så på de gamle tranene og sa: "Se, Gud har gitt dem mat hele dagen ute i sumpene. Nå drar de ned til fossen for å være sammen med sine frender for natten."
To små rødstruper, mine favorittfugler, fløy opp til reiret sitt. De hadde matet ungene sine hele dagen. Gud hadde sørget for dem. Og om natten hadde Han ordnet et trygt sted for dem å sove.
Jeg sa: "Sikkert, en dag når livet er over og solen i mitt liv går ned, og i ditt liv, har Gud også en vei for oss et sted." Det er noe som forteller oss at det er noe der ute: Dypet som kaller på dypet.

text:
I read in the paper here not long ago where a little baby eat the pedals off of a bicycle, eat the rubbers off of pencils. The doctors said what it was: his little body needed sulfur. And he was eating that rubber, because there was sulfur in the rubber. Now, before he could've--there could've been any crave for that sulfur, there had to be something in here craving it, creating that desire. And if there's something here creating that desire, there's bound to be something out there to respond to that--to that Creator, creation.
In other words like this, as I'd say this. Before you can have a desire, there has to be a Creator to create that desire. Is that right? Before there is a creation, there has to be a Creator.
text_no:
Jeg leste nylig i avisen om en liten baby som spiste pedalene på en sykkel og viskelærene på blyanter. Legene forklarte at det skyldtes at barnets kropp manglet svovel. Han spiste gummien fordi det var svovel i den. Før han kunne ha noen trang etter svovel, måtte det være noe i ham som skapte dette ønsket. Hvis det er noe i ham som skaper dette ønsket, må det nødvendigvis finnes noe der ute som kan svare på den Skaperen og skapelsen.
Med andre ord: Før du kan ha et ønske, må det være en Skaper som skaper dette ønsket. Er ikke det riktig? Før det finnes en skapelse, må det være en Skaper.

text:
And how many in here believes that there is divine healing, let's see your hands? All right. Now, if… Thank you. If you believe, and there's something in you that says there's healing somewhere, divine healing, there's bound to be a Creator to create that desire. And as sure as there's a desire in your heart to be healed by divine healing, there's a fountain open somewhere. That's all. You would--you couldn't have that desire unless there would be.
And you remember, a long time ago when the church got formal and everything, the people begin to crave more of God? The baptism of the Spirit come into existence. See, you were hungering and thirsting for more of God. And as long as you thirst for more of God, there's bound to be more of God somewhere to respond to that. If there's a deep calling, there's a deep to respond. See what I mean?
text_no:
Hvor mange her inne tror på guddommelig helbredelse? La meg se hendene deres. Greit. Takk. Hvis dere tror, og det er noe i dere som sier at det finnes guddommelig helbredelse et sted, må det nødvendigvis være en Skaper som har skapt det ønsket. Og så sant som det er et ønske i deres hjerter om å bli helbredet ved guddommelig kraft, er det en åpen kilde et sted. Det er alt. Dere kunne ikke ha hatt dette ønsket hvis ikke den kilden fantes.
Husk at for lenge siden, da menigheten ble formell og stiv, begynte folk å lengte etter mer av Gud. Da kom dåpen i Ånden til. Dere tørstet og hungret etter mer av Gud. Og så lenge dere tørster etter mer av Gud, må det nødvendigvis være mer av Gud et sted som svarer på den tørsten. Hvis det er et dypt kall, er det et dypt svar. Forstår dere hva jeg mener?

text:
Now, Simeon, he was the same way. He knew that there was a hunger in his heart. He knew that he had a promise by the Holy Ghost that he wasn't going to see death until he seen the Lord's Christ.
Now, in them days, they didn't have press and radio as we have today. So Jesus, when He was born in Bethlehem of Judea, why there was shepherds come out and worshipped and sang, and the Magis came and offered their presents and so forth. The news didn't scatter like it does now.
But on the eighth day, according to Jewish law, the woman had to come and offer the turtledove for her--and her offering for her cleansing--purification.
text_no:
Simeon hadde det på samme måte. Han visste at det var en hunger i hjertet hans. Han visste at han hadde fått et løfte fra Den Hellige Ånd om at han ikke skulle dø før han hadde sett Herrens Messias.
På den tiden hadde de ikke presse og radio slik vi har i dag. Da Jesus ble født i Betlehem i Judea, kom hyrder og tilba, og de sang, og de vise menn kom med gaver og så videre. Nyhetene spredte seg ikke som de gjør nå.
Men på den åttende dagen, ifølge jødisk lov, måtte kvinnen komme og ofre en turteldue for å gjennomgå sin renselse.

text:
Now, let's take a morning at the temple. Here's a man sitting there, back in the prayer room somewhere. It was a busy morning; maybe it was Monday morning, and everybody coming and going, thousands upon thousands.
Well, probably in the three or four million Jews there was in Jerusalem and around Palestine at that time, they'd be probably as many as fifty to a hundred babies borned every night, maybe more. Well, every morning there'd be a line of mothers line up here to offer their sacrifice and for their purifications, and offer the--according to the law.
And now, look. I see Simeon back in the temple. My, I can just notice him sitting back there, maybe reading the Scriptures. He had the promise now. And then into a door stepped… [Blank spot on tape.]
text_no:
La oss ta en morgen i tempelet. Her sitter en mann i bønnerommet et sted. Det var en travel morgen, kanskje en mandag morgen, og folk kom og gikk i tusentall.
Med tre til fire millioner jøder i Jerusalem og rundt Palestina på den tiden, ble det sannsynligvis født femti til hundre barn hver natt, kanskje flere. Hver morgen sto det en rekke mødre klare til å ofre og for å bli renhetserklært, i henhold til loven.
Nå, se. Jeg ser Simeon i tempelet. Han sitter kanskje og leser Skriftene. Han hadde fått et løfte. Og så, inn døren kommer... [Tomt.område.på.lydbånd.]

text:
I'm so thankful my name's on her book tonight, wrote by the blood of Jesus Christ across it, "Pardoned." Oh, my. You'll think I'm a holy-roller after while, sure enough. But brother, when I think of the blood of Christ that pardoned my book of sin, and throwed it in the sea of forgetfulness, it makes me get happy and rejoicing. I love Him with all my heart. If the old-time power of God that's saved me and has kept me through the age. I love Him with all my heart tonight.
And I take my stand with Paul of old. "In the way that's called heresy, so worship I the God of our fathers. I'm not ashamed of the Gospel of Jesus Christ for it is the power of God unto the salvation to those who believe." Marvelous, He's wonderful.
text_no:
Jeg er så takknemlig for at mitt navn står i Hennes bok i kveld, skrevet med Jesu Kristi blod, "Benådet." Å, min. Du vil nok tro jeg er en halleluja-ropende etter hvert. Men når jeg tenker på Kristi blod som har benådet meg fra mine synder og kastet dem i glemselens hav, fylles jeg med glede og lovprisning. Jeg elsker Ham av hele mitt hjerte. Den gamle Guds kraft som frelste meg og har bevart meg gjennom tidene, betyr alt for meg. Jeg elsker Ham av hele mitt hjerte i kveld.
Jeg tar min stand sammen med Paulus av gammel tid: "I den veien som blir kalt vranglære, slik tilber jeg våre fedres Gud. Jeg skammer meg ikke over Jesu Kristi evangelium, for det er Guds kraft til frelse for de som tror." Vidunderlig, Han er fantastisk.

text:
And I see down there, when that time come for the purification of this little mother, although with her scar and things holding in her arms (Look at her.) a Baby wrapped in swaddling's cloth. That's what they tell me, what goes on the back of a yoke of an ox that plows. And they wasn't nothing down there to wrap the little fellow in, the King of glory. What do you care how poor you are. The King of glory wrapped in swaddling's cloth, rolled Him up in that… And here's the very Prince of heaven laying in the arms of a little girl, wrapped in swaddling cloth.
There stood the rich mothers. They wouldn't get along that bunch of people. They still have that feeling. They won't get along that bunch.
And Mary stood there, and one of them walked away, said, "Look at her."
But in her heart, she knew who she was. She knew she had God's blessings. And there she had It in her arms.
Look here. That's what the natural world would think. Look around and say, "Oh, my. Look at that. That baby ain't even dressed well. And look, a peasant's offering, two turtledoves." Here stood some of them with lambs. They were rich people. But a peasant's offering…
text_no:
Da tiden kom for denne lille mors renselse, ser jeg henne der nede, med arret og alt, holding i armene sine—a et barn pakket inn i svøp. De forteller meg at det likner på tøyet som plasseres bak på et okseål når den pløyer. Det var ingenting der ned for å pakke den lille gutten inn, Kongen av herlighet. Hva gjør det om du er fattig? Kongen av herlighet pakket inn i svøp, rullet Ham opp i det… Og her er selve Himmelens Prins som ligger i armene til en liten jente, pakket inn i svøp.
De rike mødrene sto der. De ville ikke omgås den gruppen mennesker. Den følelsen har de fortsatt. De vil ikke være sammen med den gruppen.
Maria sto der, og en av dem gikk bort og sa: "Se på henne."
Men i sitt hjerte visste hun hvem hun var. Hun visste at hun hadde Guds velsignelser. Og der hadde hun Dem i armene sine.
Se her. Det er hva den naturlige verden ville tenke. Se rundt og si, "Å, min. Se på det. Den babyen er ikke engang godt kledd. Og se, et fattigmanns offer, to turtelduer." Noen av dem sto der med lam. De rike menneskene. Men et fattigmanns offer…

text:
Then while they were standing there, and the little mother keeping it all in her heart, I can see the holy Ghost move out there in the room upon that old man called Simeon, that [unclear words] "Simeon, I give you the promise, and you've lived faithful to it. Get up and start moving now."
I can see him lay down the Scroll, walk out of the building. He didn't know where he was going. He was led by the Holy Ghost. I can see him moving around through the people, come down till he hit this line of women, start down along the line. He went right to where that little scorned woman was standing there, picked up that baby wrapped in swaddling's cloth, and tears dropping off of his beard, said, "Lord, let Thy servant depart in peace, according to Thy Word, for my eyes have seen Thy Salvation." The Holy Ghost led him to the Child.
text_no:
Mens de sto der, og den lille moren beholdt alt i sitt hjerte, kan jeg se at Den Hellige Ånd beveger seg der i rommet, over mannen som het Simeon. Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Simeon, Jeg har gitt deg løftet, og du har levd trofast etter det. Stå opp og begynn å gå nå."
Jeg kan se ham legge fra seg skriftrullen, gå ut av bygningen. Han visste ikke hvor han skulle; han ble ledet av Den Hellige Ånd. Jeg kan se ham bevege seg blant folk, til han kom bort til denne rekken av kvinner. Han gikk rett bort til den lille foraktede kvinnen som sto der, løftet opp babyen innhyllet i tøy, og med tårer dryppende fra skjegget sa han: "Herre, la Din tjener fare herfra i fred, etter Ditt Ord, for mine øyne har sett Din frelse." Den Hellige Ånd ledet ham til Barnet.

text:
At the same time, an old blind prophetess sitting down there in a corner, the Holy Ghost spoke to her. And here she comes winding through them people like that: blind. Moved right up to where He was, and raised her hands and blessed God: led by the Holy Ghost, deep calling to the deep. They was expecting to see Him. There He was, the Holy Ghost leading them to it.
For years we've expected gifts to be in the church; we've expected power to be in the church. And isn't it strange tonight that you people believe in divine healing, that the Holy Ghost has led you here by the same channel, right to where God's blessings hangs over the building, ready to receive and heal anybody that's in divine presence, that'll believe it?
The same God that lived in that day, and the same Holy Ghost that led Simeon, and Anna, and them to the Christ, has led you here tonight where we're gathered together in this place in the name of the Lord Jesus to see His power move upon His people. Amen. Amen.
No, that won't scare you. Amen means "so be it." And that's right. It's the truth. How marvelous.
text_no:
Samtidig, nede i et hjørne, satt en gammel blind profetinne, og Den Hellige Ånd talte til henne. Der kommer hun, vikler seg gjennom folkemengden: blind. Hun beveget seg rett opp til Ham, løftet hendene og velsignet Gud: ledet av Den Hellige Ånd, dyp som kaller på dyp. De forventet å se Ham. Der var Han, med Den Hellige Ånd som ledet dem til Ham.
I årevis har vi forventet gaver i Menigheten; vi har forventet kraft i Menigheten. Og er det ikke merkelig i kveld at dere som tror på guddommelig helbredelse, er ledet hit av Den Hellige Ånd gjennom samme kanal, rett til der Guds velsignelser henger over bygningen, klar til å motta og helbrede alle som er i Guds nærvær og vil tro på det?
Den samme Gud som levde i den tiden, og den samme Hellige Ånd som ledet Simeon og Anna til Kristus, har ledet dere hit i kveld hvor vi er samlet i Herrens Jesu navn for å se Hans kraft virke blant Sitt folk. Amen. Amen.
Nei, det vil ikke skremme deg. Amen betyr "så være det." Og det er riktig. Det er sannheten. Så fantastisk.

text:
Do you believe in being led by the Spirit? Recently, for just for a testimony. We was having a meeting here in Fort Wayne. I'll never forget it. Many of you here was in the Fort Wayne meeting, no doubt at all. Let's see your hands who was in the Fort Wayne meeting? What a marvelous time. That's where the man was healed with the multiple sclerosis. How many was there that night he was healed, let's see your hands?
There that old man laying there, crippled up (They both may be present now.) laying like that. I didn't know. They was trying to lay the man on the platform in his white shirt there. He was a business man, had been paralyzed from his waist down for ten years in the bed. I didn't know. They… Some of his friends tried to get him up. The poor man was trying to touch my trouser leg. And they took him off the platform.
text_no:
Tror du på å bli ledet av Ånden? Jeg vil dele et vitnesbyrd med dere. Nylig hadde vi et møte i Fort Wayne som jeg aldri vil glemme. Mange av dere her var sikkert til stede på møtet i Fort Wayne. La oss se hendene til de som var på møtet i Fort Wayne. For en fantastisk tid det var. Det var der mannen med multippel sklerose ble helbredet. Hvor mange var der den kvelden han ble helbredet? La oss se hendene deres.
Den gamle mannen lå der, helt invalid. (De kan begge være til stede nå.) Jeg visste det ikke, men de prøvde å få mannen opp på plattformen i hans hvite skjorte. Han var en forretningsmann som hadde vært lam fra livet og ned i ti år og sengeliggende. Jeg visste ikke om dette. Noen av vennene hans prøvde å få ham opp. Den stakkars mannen prøvde å berøre buksebenet mitt. De bar ham bort fra plattformen.

text:
I was just sitting down. I went ahead speaking. I happened to look out, and I seen a vision of him. I knew he was going to be well. I seen him walking through some kind of a place. I spoke to him, he… And I said, "You want me to come lay my hands on you, don't you."
He said, "Yes, sir."
I walked down. The same time there, a little old man was laying there so crippled, that this man had wrote to, reached over and got a hold of my coat. He said, "Brother Branham, just lay your hands on me too while you're here, and I'll get well."
And no sooner than I touched that man there, he rose to his feet. And that people went into a scream. You remember it?
text_no:
Jeg satt nettopp ned og begynte å snakke. Jeg så tilfeldigvis ut, og jeg så en visjon av ham. Jeg visste at han kom til å bli frisk. Jeg så ham gå gjennom et slags sted. Jeg snakket til ham og sa, "Du vil at jeg skal legge hendene mine på deg, ikke sant?"
Han svarte, "Ja, sir."
Jeg gikk ned. Samtidig lå en eldre mann der, så krøplig at denne mannen, som hadde skrevet til ham, rakte ut og tok tak i frakken min. Han sa, "Bror Branham, legg hendene dine på meg også mens du er her, så blir jeg frisk."
Så snart jeg rørte ved mannen, reiste han seg. Folk begynte å skrike. Husker du det?

text:
Here a few weeks ago, I got a letter from both of them. And the man that was so paralyzed, was so crippled like this, was a plowing out in a field with his tractor. The other man [unclear words] with multiple sclerosis, came down the road and seen him. He jumped out of his car, and the man jumped off the tractor, and they run out there in the field, grabbing one another and lifting one another up and down like that. What is it? Nothing but the blood of Jesus Christ, the Son of God. He is omnipotent in His power in [unclear words]. Why, He's the same Christ yesterday, today, and forever. His powers cannot fail, because He's God. God's divine order of the earth, the Holy Spirit today is just the same as He was. To reject Jehovah was a penalty. You had… To reject Jesus was a penalty. And to reject this, what people call fanaticism, and what people call foolishness, is the Holy Ghost. He said, "To speak a word against it would never be forgiven in this world to come." It's more of a penalty to reject the Holy Spirit today than it would be to reject Jesus Christ or the Father. That's right.
Don't you be ashamed. Stand for that which is right. I don't know what's the matter with me tonight. I'm [unclear words] now. All right. Watch him.
text_no:
For noen uker siden fikk jeg et brev fra begge to. Mannen som var så lammet og krøplet, var ute og pløyde i en åker med traktoren sin. Den andre mannen, som hadde multippel sklerose, kom nedover veien og så ham. Han hoppet ut av bilen, og mannen hoppet av traktoren. De løp ut i åkeren, grep tak i hverandre og løftet hverandre opp og ned. Hva er det? Ingenting annet enn Jesu Kristi, Guds Sønns blod. Han er allmektig i Sin kraft. Hvorfor? Han er den samme Kristus i går, i dag og for alltid. Hans krefter kan ikke svikte, for Han er Gud. Guds guddommelige orden på jorden, Den Hellige Ånd, er i dag den samme som Han alltid har vært. Å avvise Jehova medførte straff. Å avvise Jesus medførte straff. Og å avvise det som folk kaller fanatisme, og det folk kaller dårskap, er å avvise Den Hellige Ånd. Han sa: "Å tale et ord mot det vil aldri bli tilgitt, verken i denne verden eller i den kommende." Det er en større straff å avvise Den Hellige Ånd i dag enn å avvise Jesus Kristus eller Faderen. Det er riktig.
Ikke vær skamfull. Stå for det som er rett. Jeg vet ikke hva som er i veien med meg i kveld. Jeg er [uklare ord] nå. Ok. Følg med.

text:
There in the meeting when things were going on. I remember that I was staying in the Indiana Hotel. And they have to keep the place a secret of where you stay, you know, because all day long when you get them sick people, that calls the anointing down, and you can't hardly get to the platform at night.
And I remember a nurse, Mayo's nurse, one of the first healings: Mrs. Margie Morgan. Cancer went through till she only weighed forty-seven pounds. Mr. Baxter and them was talking to her the other night. She weighs a hundred and fifty-five. That's been about five years ago.
The doctors said, "Oh, she'll die. She's just excited." Well, she's still excited. She's living. You better get excited then, so then it'll hold your life here a little while. Excitement, it's the power of Almighty God, not excitement. If it is, it'd be good for psychologists and doctors to go to teaching excitement. Excitement don't cure you. You know it doesn't.
text_no:
Under møtene, mens ting pågikk, husker jeg at jeg bodde på Indiana Hotel. De måtte holde stedet hvor jeg bodde hemmelig, fordi syke mennesker søkte hjelp hele dagen. Dette trakk ned salvelsen, og det var nesten umulig å nå frem til plattformen om kvelden.
Jeg husker en sykepleier fra Mayo, en av de første helbredelsene: Fru Margie Morgan. Kreften hadde herjet så mye at hun bare veide 21 kilo. Mr. Baxter og andre snakket med henne forleden kveld; nå veier hun 70 kilo. Det var for omtrent fem år siden.
Legene sa: "Åh, hun vil dø. Hun er bare opprørt." Vel, hun er fortsatt opprørt. Hun lever. Kanskje vi også burde bli opprørte, for det kan forlenge livet vårt litt. Men hennes helse er ikke et resultat av enkle følelser; det er den Allmektige Guds kraft. Hvis følelser kunne kurere, ville det vært bra for psykologer og leger å begynne å undervise i følelser. Følelser kurerer deg ikke. Det vet vi.

text:
Now, notice. We was staying in the hotel. And the little bellhop come up, and he said, "Reverend Branham, I'm sorry to inform you, but you couldn't even get out that door this morning." Said, "That auditorium's packed full. They know you're here."
We waited awhile. And no way to get out to breakfast. So after while, another one come up, said, "I'll tell you; I can take you through the basement if you don't mind, your wife and them, climb over some ashes."
I said, "I don't mind."
We went down, went through a big pile of ashes, and come out into an alley. We started down to the little place called Toddle House. You remember where that's at? There's where Mr. Eaton was healed that morning with stomach trouble when he run up there to pay for my breakfast.
text_no:
Legg merke til dette. Vi bodde på hotellet, og den lille pikkoloen kom opp og sa: "Pastor Branham, jeg beklager å informere deg, men du kan ikke engang komme deg ut av døren i morges. Auditoriet er fullt. De vet at du er her."
Vi ventet en stund, men det var ingen måte å komme ut for å spise frokost. Etter en stund kom en annen pikkolo opp og sa: "Jeg kan ta dere gjennom kjelleren hvis du ikke har noe imot det, du og din kone, og de andre må klatre over noen askehoper."
Jeg sa, "Det gjør ingenting."
Vi gikk ned, gjennom en stor askehaug, og kom ut i en bakgate. Vi satte kursen mot et lite sted som heter Toddle House. Husker du hvor det er? Det var der Mr. Eaton ble helbredet den morgenen for mageproblemer da han løp opp for å betale frokosten min.

text:
And I remember, I had my overcoat up like this, walking down the street a holding my little girl, Mrs. Morgan on one side, and my wife on the other. We were walking down the street. I started to cross the street to go to the Toddle House, where we were eating our breakfast and our meals. And just then, I felt the Holy Spirit. You believe in being led by the Spirit now? All right. Listen closely then. It said, "Turn this a way."
And I started to turn. Mrs. Morgan said, "Where you going?"
My wife looked quickly said, "Spirit leading now. Just watch. Just follow him."
And went down the street. I don't know where I went, but after while, I stopped and looked up. It said, "Miller's Cafeteria." I went in. (These people may be sitting here tonight for all I know.) I went in. I got a cup of, I believe, of chocolate or something, sat down to drink it, Mrs. Morgan, all of them.
When we asked the blessing, I raised my head. I said something to my little girl. I seen a woman raise up. She said, "Bless God. Bless God."
I thought… Mrs. Morgan turned around, said, "You're trapped in here now."
I said, "Mrs. Morgan, don't say that." See? I said, "This is the Holy Spirit."
text_no:
Jeg husker at jeg gikk nedover gaten med frakken trukket opp slik, mens jeg holdt på min lille jente. Fru Morgan var på den ene siden og min kone på den andre. Vi gikk nedover gaten og skulle krysse for å gå til Toddle House, hvor vi spiste frokost og andre måltider. Akkurat da kjente jeg Den Hellige Ånd. Tror du på å bli ledet av Ånden? Greit, lytt nøye da. Det sa: "Gå denne veien."
Jeg begynte å snu meg. Fru Morgan spurte: "Hvor skal du?"
Min kone sa raskt: "Ånden leder nå. Bare se. Bare følg ham."
Vi fortsatte nedover gaten. Jeg visste ikke hvor jeg gikk, men etter en stund stoppet jeg og så opp. Det sto "Miller's Cafeteria". Jeg gikk inn. (Disse menneskene kan være her i kveld for alt jeg vet.) Jeg gikk inn og fikk en kopp med, jeg tror det var sjokolade eller noe, og satte meg ned for å drikke det sammen med Fru Morgan og alle de andre.
Da vi ba bordsbønnen, løftet jeg hodet og sa noe til min lille jente. Jeg så en kvinne reise seg og si: "Lovet være Gud. Lovet være Gud."
Jeg tenkte… Fru Morgan snudde seg og sa: "Du er fanget her nå."
Jeg sa: "Fru Morgan, ikke si det." Ser du? Jeg sa: "Dette er Den Hellige Ånd."

text:
This woman come over there wringing her hands. She said, "Brother Branham, I'm from Texas." She said, "My brother is dying with heart trouble." Said, "We followed. This is about eight or ten meetings that we've been into, and we've never been able to get a prayer card that was called into the line." And said, "To get up here this time, we sold our cow," said, "because my… The doctor said my brother could only live a little while longer." The heart, something had injured the diaphragm. Said, "He's just got a few more weeks to live. And it was our last chance." Said, "We [unclear words] till last night. We can't even get in to get--to the place any more." And said, "I prayed all night long." And she said, "This morning just about four o'clock in the morning," she said, "I had a dream. And in this dream it told me to come down and find a place by the name of Miller's Cafeteria, and to be here at nine o'clock and my brother'd be healed."
I looked at my watch, and exactly nine o'clock. You know what took place. Yes, sir. And that boy was healed.
text_no:
En kvinne kom bort dit mens hun vred hendene sine. Hun sa, "Bror Branham, jeg er fra Texas." Hun sa, "Min bror er døende av hjerteproblemer." Hun sa, "Vi har fulgt deg. Dette er omtrent åtte eller ti møter vi har deltatt på, og vi har aldri klart å få et bønnekort som ble kalt inn i bønnekøen." Hun sa, "For å komme hit denne gangen, solgte vi kua vår," og la til, "fordi legen sa at min bror bare hadde kort tid igjen å leve." Hjertet, noe hadde skadet diafragmaen. Hun sa, "Han har bare noen få uker igjen å leve. Dette var vårt siste forsøk." Hun sa, "Vi [uklare ord] til i går kveld. Vi kan ikke engang komme inn i lokalet mer." Hun sa, "Jeg ba hele natten." Og hun sa, "I morges rundt klokken fire," sa hun, "hadde jeg en drøm. Og i denne drømmen ble jeg fortalt å dra til et sted ved navn Miller's Cafeteria, og være der klokken ni for at min bror skulle bli helbredet."
Jeg så på klokken min, og den var nøyaktig ni. Du vet hva som skjedde. Ja, herr. Og den gutten ble helbredet.

text:
I turned, and got my hat, and started out the door, and people begin screaming. That cafeteria was alive in a few minutes. I started to walk out the door. Said something, a blessing to them, started to walk out the door, and a little woman… She was at San Bernardino a few weeks ago to testify to me, was coming back from Brazil to see her daughter, which is a missionary.
And she fell down on the street and grabbed a hold of my legs like that. She said, "Oh, God…"
I picked her up, and I said, "What's the matter, sister?"
Meda, my wife and them, they started picking her up. Said, "What's the matter?"
And she was way big, malignant. And Mayo Brothers had turned her down. Her husband owns the spaghetti company here in Chicago. And she was a little woman. And she was, oh, so swollen out. She said, "Brother Branham," she said, "it's my last chance." She said, "Oh, I've just prayed and cried." Said, "Mayo's has turned me down, and there's nothing could be done about this here. I've taken radium; I've taken x-ray treatments, and everything, and nothing could be done." And she said, "This morning, just along about the break of day," she said, "I was awakened. I was looking out. And I went back to sleep again." Said, "I dreamed a dream that I was to stand in front of Miller's Cafeteria ten minutes after nine to be healed." Oh, my. Do you believe in leading by the Spirit? Yes, sir.
text_no:
Jeg snudde meg, tok hatten min og gikk mot døren. Folk begynte å skrike. Etter noen minutter var kafeen i fullt liv. Jeg begynte å gå ut døren, sa en velsignelse til dem og startet å gå ut igjen da en liten kvinne... Hun var ved San Bernardino for noen uker siden for å vitne for meg. Hun kom tilbake fra Brasil for å besøke datteren sin, som er misjonær.
Hun falt om på gaten og grep tak i beina mine. Hun sa: "Å, Gud..." 
Jeg løftet henne opp og sa: "Hva er i veien, søster?"
Meda, kona mi, og noen andre begynte å hjelpe henne opp. De spurte: "Hva er galt?"
Hun hadde en stor, ondartet svulst. Mayo Brothers hadde avvist henne. Ektemannen hennes eier et spaghetti-firma her i Chicago. Hun var en liten kvinne, men så oppsvulmet. Hun sa: "Bror Branham, dette er min siste sjanse. Jeg har bedt og grått. Mayo har avvist meg, og det finnes ingen løsning. Jeg har tatt radium- og røntgenbehandling, men ingenting har hjulpet." Hun sa: "I morges, rett ved daggry, våknet jeg. Jeg så ut, og så sovnet jeg igjen. Jeg drømte at jeg skulle stå foran Miller's Cafeteria ti minutter over ni for å bli helbredet." Å, du milde. Tror du på ledelse av Ånden? Ja, sir.

text:
On down the street I went. Just a little while longer. (And you people from Fort Wayne and across the line know of this.) I was going down the street. My wife said, "What… Are you ready to go in now?"
I said, "I don't know." We got down there, and I seen some fishing tackle. And I started to turn for that. Just then the Holy Spirit moved down again and begin to move over. This is my inside life I'm telling you about. It's happened right here in this city too, today. And then here It moving around. I felt It. I said, "Oh, It's again." I said, "Now, honey, you go on over and get the … at the drug store over there and get that little coloring book for the baby." Had to keep her in a room all day. I said, "You all go on back in. I don't know when I'm going to be able to … the Holy Spirit will relieve me now."
text_no:
Jeg fortsatte nedover gaten. Bare litt lenger. (Dere som er fra Fort Wayne og omegn kjenner til dette.) Vi gikk nedover gaten da min kone spurte: "Er du klar til å gå inn nå?"
Jeg svarte: "Jeg vet ikke." Vi kom til et sted hvor jeg så fiskeutstyr, og jeg begynte å gå mot det. Akkurat da kom Den Hellige Ånd igjen og begynte å røre seg over meg. Dette er mitt indre liv jeg forteller om. Det har skjedd her i denne byen også, i dag. Jeg følte Den igjen og sa: "Å, det skjer igjen." Så sa jeg: "Nå, kjære, du går bort til apoteket der borte og kjøper den lille fargeboken til babyen." Vi måtte holde henne inne hele dagen. Jeg sa: "Dere kan gå tilbake inn. Jeg vet ikke når Den Hellige Ånd vil gi meg ro nå."

text:
I went on down at the… Started down at the end of the street. And I walked up there and begin to look towards that tackle and stuff. And I got back to the side; I said, "Heavenly Father, where would You have Your servant go?" I thought maybe He wants me to stand here a little bit. And I stood a little bit; nothing happened. I thought, "Well…" I started to walk towards the corner.
He said, "Go across the other corner."
And the cop blowed the whistle there. And I went across the other side, and stood there a little bit, about ten minutes. I didn't know why I was standing there. People passed. I was just standing there. That's all I knowed. When you're led of the Spirit, God wants you to just do what He says do. He's the One on the other end of the line doing the work.
text_no:
Jeg gikk nedover gaten og begynte å se på utstyret der. Så trakk jeg meg litt tilbake og sa: "Himmelske Far, hvor vil Du at Din tjener skal gå?" Jeg tenkte kanskje Han ville at jeg skulle stå der en stund. Jeg ble stående litt, men ingenting skjedde. Jeg tenkte: "Vel..." og begynte å gå mot hjørnet.
Han sa: "Gå over til det andre hjørnet."
En politimann blåste i fløyten, og jeg gikk over til den andre siden. Jeg sto der i omtrent ti minutter uten å vite hvorfor jeg ventet. Folk gikk forbi, og jeg bare sto der. Alt jeg visste var at når du blir ledet av Ånden, ønsker Gud at du skal gjøre som Han sier. Han er den som gjør arbeidet.

text:
I just stood there a little bit. And I thought, "Oh, what is going to happen?"
And a big Irish cop out there blew the whistle again; the pedestrians crossed. And coming behind was a little woman with a checkered dress on, a checkered tam, looked like a Canadian. She was packing a pocketbook on her arm. She had her head down. She went walking along like this, and she…
The Holy Spirit said, "Stand over here."
And she passed that close to me, just went right on by. I thought, "Why is that?" She went a little farther. I thought, "Well, that's strange. And He told me to stand here by her. Wonder why. Maybe she just caught the blessing as went by like that."
text_no:
Jeg sto der en liten stund og tenkte: "Hva kommer til å skje?"
En stor irsk politimann borte ved siden blåste i fløyta igjen, og fotgjengerne krysset gaten. Bak dem kom en liten kvinne i en rutete kjole og en rutete lue, som så ut som en kanadier. Hun bar en veske på armen og gikk med hodet senket.
Den Hellige Ånd sa: "Stå her."
Hun passerte meg så nær at hun nesten rørte ved meg og fortsatte videre. Jeg tenkte: "Hvorfor det?" Hun gikk litt lenger bort, og jeg undret meg: "Hva var det som var så merkelig? Han ba meg stå her ved henne. Jeg lurer på hvorfor. Kanskje hun fikk velsignelsen da hun gikk forbi."

text:
In a few moments she turned around, and said, "Oh," she said, "Brother Branham…" And she come back. She said, "I don't… I'm beside myself."
I said, "I don't think so, sister. What's the matter?"
She said, "I'm from Canada. I'm only allowed a hundred and fifty dollars a year to come over here." She said, "I--I spent all my money." And she said, "I--I--I was in the hotel lobby, and I had five cents for coffee this morning." She said, "And I was on my road back to hitchhike back to Canada." Said, "I couldn't get in the lines, and I was praying." And said, "Something just turned me around and made me come up two or three blocks and walk across this a way." Oh, my. There He is.
I seen her holding her hand. I said, "Is it your arm, sister?"
She said, "Yes, I fell on a rock. I was riding a dog when I was a little girl, and I fell on a rock and hurt this arm like this." And while she was talking, here come her arm coming out like that. She begin screaming the top of her voice.
And that big cop said, "I know who you are, Brother Branham."
You talk about a prayer line, we had it all up-and-down the street there for a while, the people standing, moving. Oh, how marvelous.
text_no:
Etter noen øyeblikk snudde hun seg rundt og sa: «Åh, Bror Branham…» Hun kom tilbake og sa: «Jeg vet ikke… Jeg er ute av meg selv.»
Jeg sa: «Jeg tror ikke det, søster. Hva er i veien?»
Hun sa: «Jeg er fra Canada. Jeg har bare lov til å bruke hundre og femti dollar i året for å komme hit.» Hun fortsatte: «Jeg har brukt opp alle pengene mine. Jeg hadde fem cent til kaffe i hotellobbyen i morges.» Hun sa videre: «Jeg var på vei tilbake for å haike til Canada. Jeg kunne ikke komme meg inn i køene, så jeg ba.» Og så sa hun: «Noe bare snudde meg rundt og fikk meg til å gå to eller tre kvartaler og gå hit.» Åh, min. Der Er Han.
Jeg så henne holde hånden sin. Jeg spurte: «Er det armen din, søster?»
Hun svarte: «Ja, jeg falt på en stein. Jeg red på en hund da jeg var liten jente, og jeg falt på en stein og skadet denne armen slik.» Og mens hun snakket, begynte armen hennes å bli helbredet. Hun begynte å skrike så høyt hun kunne.
Den store politimannen sa: «Jeg vet hvem du er, Bror Branham.»
Du kan tro vi hadde en bønnekø oppe og ned langs gaten en stund, med folk stående og bevegende seg. Åh, hvor vidunderlig.

text:
Here not long ago, I was coming from Dallas, Texas, in spring, and I thought… I was flying back, and I was grounded at Memphis, right where my… Had to be one night on the fifth at Memphis, Tennessee. I was grounded there, and they put me up there in that, I believe, the Peabody Hotel, it's called. It's a very nice place. Myself, I couldn't afford to go in it, but the airlines sent me up there. And I remember that morning, they called me, said, "Now, the plane will leave at eight o'clock, Reverend Branham." Said, "They'll be by to pick you up."
I said, "Yes, sir." And I got my letters ready and was going down to mail the letters. Now, listen closely, I'm going to close in a minute. And I thought, "Oh, my, I'll go down and mail these letters." And I got out and started down the street, put--trying to find the Post Office. And I was walking down the street singing that little Pentecostal song, let's see:
Telling now, both far and wide,
His power is yet the same,
I'm so glad that I can say I'm one of them.
Brother Ryan, you remember, when you used to sing it. And saying, "We…" And going singing that little song. And after while, I felt something going, "Whooo…" got close. I stepped back up behind a great big column there, and I said, "Heavenly Father, what would You have Your servant do?" It kept going, "Whooo…" I was real still a minute, and I heard a deep voice say, "Turn, go back."
text_no:
For ikke lenge siden kom jeg fra Dallas, Texas, om våren. Jeg skulle fly tilbake og ble værfast i Memphis, der jeg måtte tilbringe natten, på det jeg tror heter Peabody Hotel. Det er et veldig fint sted. Selv hadde jeg ikke råd til å bo der, men flyselskapet sørget for oppholdet. Jeg husker at de ringte meg neste morgen og sa: "Flyet går klokken åtte, Reverend Branham. Noen vil hente deg."
Jeg svarte: "Ja, sir." Jeg gjorde klar brevene mine for å sende dem. Nå, hør nøye etter, jeg skal avslutte om et øyeblikk. Jeg tenkte: "Å, jeg må ned og sende disse brevene." Så jeg gikk ut og begynte å lete etter postkontoret mens jeg gikk nedover gaten og sang en liten pinse-sang:
"Fortell nå, både vidt og bredt,
Hans makt er fortsatt den samme,
Jeg er så glad at jeg kan si at jeg er en av dem."
Bror Ryan, du husker når du pleide å synge den. Så jeg gikk nedover gata og sang denne lille sangen. Etter en stund kjente jeg noe komme nærmere, nesten som en "Whooo...". Jeg trakk meg tilbake bak en stor søyle og sa: "Himmelske Far, hva vil Du at Din tjener skal gjøre?" Det holdt på med "Whooo…" Jeg stod stille et øyeblikk og hørte en dyp stemme si: "Vend, gå tilbake."

text:
Well, I turned and went walking back down the street. I walked. And I walked on past the hotel, on down through south Memphis, just walking on and on. The sun was way high then. The sun was up, and it was a beautiful spring morning. The fragrance of the flowers in the air, had perfumed the air.
I thought, "How beautiful." Was going walking along there. I thought, "Lord, I don't know. You said, 'Walk.' Here I am. I'm walking." I just kept on walking, walking, walking. Walked out pretty near the other side of Memphis. And I remember going down a little hill towards where a little creek of a thing ran through this way, a little tributary there at the river of some kind. I just went walking down through there. There's was a little old whitewashed hut down there; a lot of colored people lived down in that way. I was walking down through there, singing--humming to myself,
Only believe, only believe.
Satan said, "You're going to miss your plane."
I said, "I'll walk."
All things are possible.
Don't let him tell you a lie. He's a liar. When God is a leading, you go do what God said do. Yes, sir. He tells you you're healed; believe it, and just start walking on. And I just kept on like that, singing,
Only Believe [unclear words].
text_no:
Jeg snudde meg og begynte å gå tilbake nedover gaten. Jeg gikk og gikk forbi hotellet, videre gjennom sør-Memphis. Solen sto høyt på himmelen. Det var en vakker vårmorgen, og duften av blomster fylte luften.
Jeg tenkte, "Så vakkert." Jeg gikk langs veien og tenkte, "Herre, jeg vet ikke. Du sa, 'Gå.' Her er jeg. Jeg går." Jeg fortsatte å gå, gå, gå. Jeg gikk nesten til den andre siden av Memphis. Jeg husker jeg gikk ned en liten bakke mot en bekk som rant gjennom området, en liten sideelv til en større elv. Jeg gikk ned dit. Der sto en liten, hvitemalt hytte. Flere fargede bodde der. Jeg gikk ned dit, nynnet for meg selv og sang:
"Only believe, only believe."
Satan sa, "Du kommer til å miste flyet ditt."
Jeg sa, "Jeg vil gå."
"All things are possible."
Ikke la ham lyve for deg. Han er en løgner. Når Gud leder, må du gjøre som Gud sier. Ja, sir. Når Han sier du er helbredet, tro det og begynn å gå. Jeg fortsatte å synge:
"Only Believe."

text:
I was noticing, leaning across a gate like this, there was a typical old Aunt Jemima, great big heavy set colored woman with a man's shirt tied around her head. She was leaning out across there. I kind of quit singing, humming, when I was far as that wall from her. I just went walking on by.
